model.label: Gemini 3 Flash
model.description: Google DeepMind • Fast Gemini model optimized for low-latency reasoning, multimodal inputs, and tool use.

pick.rationale: Saint Mary's enters this matchup with a superior analytical profile, boasting a top-25 defense that is well-equipped to slow down Texas A&M's high-octane offense. While the Aggies possess SEC-level athleticism, they face a significant size disadvantage in the paint and lack the defensive consistency to match the Gaels' disciplined execution. Saint Mary's ability to control the tempo and dominate the glass should allow them to neutralize Texas A&M's scoring threats and advance to the next round.

reasoningStep.title: Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Saint Mary's holds a clear edge in defensive efficiency and interior size, which should counteract Texas A&M's offensive pace and athleticism.
evidence: Saint Mary's ranks 24th in KenPom and 25th in Torvik, while Texas A&M ranks 39th in both.
Saint Mary's boasts the 22nd-ranked adjusted defensive efficiency nationally.
Reports indicate a significant size mismatch favoring Saint Mary's interior players against a smaller Texas A&M frontcourt.
Saint Mary's finished the season 27-5 with a top-20 scoring defense (64.6 PA/G).

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
